steps.title: Keep the list parallel
steps.content: The sentence lists examples from three fields. Each item should begin with a field marker: "in astronomy," "in chemistry," and "in mathematics."

steps.title: Introduce the examples
steps.content: B places "in astronomy" before the colon and breaks the pattern, C uses only a comma to introduce the examples, and D uses a semicolon incorrectly. A colon after "abound" lets all three field phrases begin parallel examples. The answer is A.
